heading: ALLOCATION OF REVENUE: CERTAIN MUNICIPALITIES
operative_text: Notwithstanding any other provision of this chapter, a home-rule municipality that borders on the Gulf of Mexico and has a population of more than 250,000 may use all or any portion of the revenue derived from the municipal hotel occupancy tax from hotels in an area previously subject to a county hotel occupancy tax and located on an island bordering the Gulf of Mexico to clean and maintain public beaches in the municipality.

defined_terms.definition_excerpt: has the meaning assigned by Section 61.063, Natural Resources Code.
has the meaning assigned by Section 61.001, Natural Resources Code.
means beach patrol, lifeguard services, marine water safety, and park law enforcement.
has the meaning assigned by Section 33.601, Natural Resources Code.

_note: Operative text captures subsection (b) as the primary operative provision; subsections (c), (d), and (e) contain additional operative rules for other municipality classes. Full text is complete and not truncated.
